title: Advanced TypeScript: Interfaces & Generics

questions.question_text: Which TypeScript feature allows a function to work with multiple data types while maintaining strict type safety?
questions.explanation: Generics allow you to create reusable components that work with a variety of types rather than a single one.

options: A way to create reusable components/functions that work with different types while maintaining safety
A new way to write loops
questions.explanation: Think of Generics as 'passing a type as an argument' to a function or class.

questions.question_text: What does the 'Partial<T>' utility type do?
options: Returns a new type where all properties of T are optional
Allows only part of the functions to run
questions.explanation: Extremely useful for update functions where you only send changed fields.

questions.question_text: What does the 'Readonly<T>' utility type do?
options: Prevents the properties of an object from being modified after creation
Changing a variable from string to number
Telling the TS compiler 'I know more than you' about the type of a value (using 'as')
A type that combines multiple types into one, requiring all properties from both
A type that is shared between many files
questions.explanation: Used to merge interfaces or types together.

options: A type that is a specific, exact value (like 'red' | 'blue' instead of any string)
A type that is very long
A type that is only text
A safer alternative to 'any' for values whose type we don't know yet (requires a check before use)
questions.explanation: You must check the type (narrowing) before you can call methods on an 'unknown' variable.

options: Constructs an object type with specific set of keys K and values of type T
Saves a record to the database
questions.explanation: Ideal for mapping IDs to specific objects.

options: Using a common property (a 'tag' or 'kind') to narrow down which specific type in a union is being used
Deleting types that are the same
questions.explanation: Essential for pattern matching in state management logic.

options: Finds the key to a house
Returns a union of the keys (property names) of an object type
questions.explanation: Example: If `User` has `name` and `age`, `keyof User` is `'name' | 'age'`.

questions.question_text: What does the 'Pick<T, K>' utility do?
options: Allows the user to pick a color
Creates a new type by choosing (picking) specific keys K from an existing type T
questions.explanation: Allows you to reuse parts of a larger interface.

options: Creates a new type by REMOVING specific keys K from an existing type T
An array with a fixed number of elements where each element has a specific type
A variable that can only be 2

questions.question_text: How do you constrain a Generic so it MUST have a specific property (like .length)?
questions.explanation: The 'extends' keyword targets specific structure requirements for the generic.

options: Breaking a tuple apart into variables
questions.explanation: Works just like JS array destructuring but with type safety for each position.
